exclusion_criteria.criterion: Solely retrieval OR solely generation: focuses strictly on IR techniques or purely generative models without explicit retrieval-augmented integration.
Minimal discussion of RAG: retrieval+generation is only peripheral/superficial and lacks substantial results or analyses.
Non-substantive publication: very short abstract, publicity note, or material without verifiable methodology.

required.criterion: Relevant to RAG or closely related baselines: clearly integrates a retriever with a generative language model OR uses retrieval to supply context to a generator.
Centers on knowledge-intensive tasks (e.g., open-domain QA, fact-checking, knowledge-grounded dialogue, or other tasks benefiting from external document retrieval).

summary_topics.description: A Systematic Review of Key Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

topic: A Systematic Review of Key Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
topic_definition: Retrieval-augmented generation is an architecture that combines external information retrieval with generative language models so that outputs are conditioned on retrieved evidence rather than only on parametric model memory.
